title: Elatine fruticosa
intro: Elatine fruticosa, also known as water wort, is a perennial aquatic plant that thrives in freshwater environments, characterized by its small, green leaves and delicate purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Elatine fruticosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Elatine fruticosa is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of herbal teas, tinctures, and topical creams.
taxonomy: Elatine fruticosa belongs to the family Elatinaceae. It is a perennial aquatic plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. Elatine fruticosa is classified as Elatine fruticosa within the genus Elatine. The plant is further classified in the kingdom Plantae and in the phylum Magnoliophyta. Elatine fruticosa is also known to be a member of the class Magnoliopsida.
